homePage.title: Tomás Gonzalez Humphreys
homePage.description: Product-driven Software Engineer building complex applications from concept to production. I thrive in fast-paced environments, engineering full-stack products—from data-intensive automation pipelines to serverless e-commerce platforms—using Next.js, TypeScript, Python, and Node.js. Currently at Deloitte (Meta client), I own E2E automation pipelines that process millions of financial records, bridging the gap between non-technical stakeholders and scalable architectures to save 10+ hours monthly. I actively leverage AI tooling (Claude Code, ChatGPT) to accelerate delivery, focusing on building systems that are accurate, scalable, and user-centric.

about.title: About Me
about.description:
I am a software engineer who likes to generate impact by creating software solutions and solving problems with critical thinking.
Currently, I am working in the tax sector for a Big Tech client, leading automations for processing millions of records and saving over 10 hours of work for the business with Python solutions and Artificial Intelligence workflows. Despite working at a Big Four, I took end-to-end ownership of each implementation and took the lead and protagonism of the project (as the sole dev on the team), connecting and gathering requirements with the operational team, consulting managers, and Big Tech client managers. I have the appropriate understanding and context to work with large volumes of data, which is of great importance, since in taxes, every penny counts.
On the other hand, I continue to improve and launch full-stack web products and solutions, such as a Full-stack Next.js E-commerce, which I developed from end to end and where I implemented Next.js best practices, using App Router, Server Actions, integrations with payment methods, logistics, Clerk authentication, Google Analytics, and making the web a PWA so that administrators receive order notifications. Furthermore, I integrated a complete CI/CD pipeline involving Linting, Testing, and deployment with Docker on Fly.io, since it has servers in Brazil and represents lower latency for the Latin American market. Additionally, I am a profile that gets 100% involved in the problems I have to solve, and I prove it by analyzing every Google Search Console metric, ranking first in Google search, and optimizing GEO and SEO.
Additionally, I have collaborative projects where I played important roles and took end-to-end charge of specific functionalities and integrations, such as in the MGRC PWA app, where I was involved in the integration of the Hockey fixture and results, sticker album features, and more. Also in FutDraft, a university project that is going to production, I led the multi-layer backend architecture in Node.js + Express.js + Socket.io for the real-time game, in addition to some key frontend features. I also took the e-commerce of my own venture, Puros Mates, to production, implementing Java + Spring Boot on the backend, with Next.js on the frontend. In these last projects, I had the opportunity to work collaboratively and in a team, something I love and enjoy very much.
I accelerate my day-to-day development with artificial intelligence tools like Claude Code, Google Antigravity, and Opencode, prioritizing best practices, optimizing tokens, and constantly reviewing, allowing me to reach production in a fraction of the time it should cost.
My preferred stack is Next.js with Shadcn for the frontend, and for the backend, Next.js Server Actions or API Routes themselves (facilitated by a monorepo), or Node.js + Express.js. At work, I build monolithic applications with Flet and Python using Pandas, Polars, DuckDB, and SQL Server. I have experience creating CI/CD workflows, ensuring a safe deployment to production with ESLint, Jest, and Docker.
I like working in dynamic environments, where the goal is to solve complex problems as quickly and as well as possible. I am currently improving my skills with AWS and perfecting my aforementioned stack.
I have a very fluent level of English, holding a full IB diploma and the FCE at a B2 level. As seen in my background, I am a person who likes to step out of my comfort zone and accept new challenges. Even though I may have a preferred stack, I like to solve problems with the best possible tools, and thanks to my foundations, I consider myself capable of picking up any tools that are needed.

items.title: Puros Mates – E-commerce
description: Full-stack Ecommerce made with Next.js, with integrated payments and logistics.
Ensuring SEO and excellent load times, to rank in search engines and GEO (appearing in AI chat recommendations)
External API integration like Mercado Pago with API Routes, users with Clerk (Google) and push notifications in PWA
Complete admin panel for autonomous E-commerce management and analytics tools to measure store performance with Shadcn UI
Ownership and commitment to delivering a robust and scalable solution
Push Notifications in PWA using FCM to notify administrators of new orders
Frontend deployment on Vercel, with personalized domain for more user trust
There are still improvements, but the development guarantees a ready-to-market product, which will be the main growth tool for the business

items.title: MGRC App
description: Website with team alignment, results and standings of the Monte Grande Rugby Club, both Rugby and Hockey
My role was to take ownership of some key features, like the visualization of results and standings for the 8 Hockey teams
I worked collectively with other colleagues where I was able to grow a lot in my teamwork
The project still has pending features like sticker album integration for the club, game in 'Gran DT' format and QR scanning for club access
Development and maintenance of internal Python tools for processing millions of records, prioritizing data correctness, validations and reproducible results.
Design of automated pipelines for data ingestion, transformation and aggregation using Pandas, Polars, DuckDB and SQL Server, integrating multiple encrypted Excel sources.
Implementation of end-to-end solutions, from requirements gathering with stakeholders to deployment in corporate environments.
Translation of business rules into deterministic and traceable technical logic, used in critical accounting processes and operational reports.

I worked as the only dev on the implementation of several projects and improvements, like Excel improvements using Macros and VBA, solving errors in legacy Tableau dashboards and improving and innovating with a product that saved time for the operational team
This gave me quite a bit of fluency in gathering business requirements, having communications with both management and operational levels, and solving problems in the best possible way

items.role: Product Engineer / Full Stack Developer | Independent Ventures
description: E2E Product Ownership: Responsible for the complete lifecycle (from concept to production) of real e-commerce platforms (Puros Mates and Vela Vida), prioritizing conversion and user experience.

Full-Stack Development with Next.js: Implementation of high-performance interfaces using Next.js (App Router) and React, optimizing server-side rendering (SSR) to maximize SEO and achieve Core Web Vitals metrics.
Critical Business Integrations: Design and execution of complex checkout flows, integrating payment gateways (Mercado Pago) and logistics services to automate commercial operations.
Growth and SEO Strategy: Direct management of organic positioning and information architecture, getting physical products to real users efficiently through the web.
'Founder' Vision: Decision-making on the tech stack based on time-to-market and final product stability.
Focus on improving user experience in every corner of the platform, being able to make a purchase in just 4 clicks.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: If you're building a product and need someone who can work on backend, data and frontend, reach out. If you're a recruiter and want to check my Github repositories for these projects, contact me and I'll share them with you.
